replies.author: @MAGAMAN
replies.text: AI movies are a very long distance away. Very, very long distance. I have been using AI daily for months and getting the AI to understand what you want is extremely difficult and there is Zero repeatability. If you create something and want to make a small change, the entire scene will change, even using a starter image. Sometimes telling AI to not do something will cause it to do something because the only thing that registers is the word you told it not to do. Having two people on screen while talking will result in one person saying part of the line and the other one saying the last part, more often than not. Having two people on camera having a conversation is pretty much impossible. Try and get an AI to do a video of someone throwing a spear, or having spears flying by them. Hahahaha.  I use numerous different AI models from the cheap to the very expensive and there is always something wrong with what they generate. It is basically a slot machine where you roll until you get something barely acceptable. If you want the scenery, the people and the actions to be exact, forget it.

author: @Jockolantern
text:
Not a single false jump scare in the entire movie which I found entirely refreshing.  Follows the original concept, setting and gameplay execution of the indie horror game quite faithfully (love the 1:1 recreation if the submersible's control panel) while expanding things in sensible ways which help flesh out the player character motivations, behind the scenes events and adding set pieces that feel like deleted areas, events or concepts which could have been in the original game.

I quite like what he and his DP pulled off in terms of camera shots:  Clever, varied shot placement, nice focus pulls.  The special effects are fine for an indie affair like this and the music/sound design blend together to set the mood well.

It's nothing amazing but it's well executed and an original property to boot not tied down by franchise expectations or studio meddling.

Mark did a good job.  Hope he is able to do something bigger and better with the money he earns from this.

author: @Bsmitbs
text: Markiplier fan here: Mark has done something that Hollywood hasn't done in years. (Spoilers ahead for the movie) He's given us 3d characters. They have flaws both good and bad. This horror movie isn't meant to be like all the other ones out here. It's about the fear of the unkown which Mark talks about in his videos is what makes a horror game GOOD. You are left pondering and wondering WHAT is out there. It's not meant to be a sequel spitter cash grab. Many people don't like it for that. But I enjoy that we finally have some NEW never done before media. It's not the same video game jumanji adventures. Mark also seeks for good in there too. He asked anyone who went to the movie to go donate blood while they were out- for the worldwide blood shortage. I encourage anyone else seeing the movie to go donate as well. Hes also confirmed hes working with Blu-ray to get it on DVD, so none if that "buying isnt owning now you have to have 20 million subscriptions just to watch it." Type deal. Much love and I hope his movie gets SO much more attention

replies.author: @JohnDoe-c8w3p
replies.text:
@MAGAMAN first of all the $15 million isn’t profit. 

Secondly, $15 million for 4,000 theaters is only $3,750 per theater, which is only 250 tickets sold over a 3 day period… which means only 125 people saw that movie a day.

Which is nothing.

On top of that Markiplier is maybe, maybe, getting half of that. 

So, again, nothing.
